mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 9.5% signals improving labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low UNRATE regimes fall below 0.5% in 40%+ of cases, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining core inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a strong economy with disinflationary pressures, creating a 'Goldilocks' environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to explain >15% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or 60-day returns decline in 60%+ of co-movement cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-quarter rebound in real GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding demand-side momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a 20-day IC < 0.15, or 60-day returns decline despite demand-side expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates that depress S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of high-inflation periods, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns falls below -0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month surge in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200k signals robust labor market demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS environments fall below 0.3% in 50%+ of cases, or the predictive power of PAYEMS drops below 0.15 IC.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) during rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates demand destruction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS fails to explain >10% of SPX return variance, or 20-day returns remain positive despite rate hikes and sales declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in 50%+ of INDPRO decline periods, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns drops below -0.2.
